claims-intel-default.label: Claims Intel Default
claims-intel-default.editorialIntent: Fast, credible claims intelligence for veterans reading on mobile.
claims-intel-default.toneEmphasis: Lead with the practical consequence before the background.
mustDo: Anchor every major claim to a regulation, case, policy memo, or docket-supported fact.
Translate legal jargon into plain English the first time it appears.
Keep paragraphs short and make next steps explicit.
avoid: Long legal-history detours before explaining why the issue matters now.
Unnamed court-ruling framing when the dossier lacks a verified case.

cavc-analysis.label: CAVC Analysis
cavc-analysis.editorialIntent: Case-driven appellate analysis for veterans, advocates, and attorneys.
cavc-analysis.toneEmphasis: Precise, citation-heavy, and careful about the actual holding.
mustDo: Name the case and docket when verified.
Separate the holding from dicta and downstream strategy.
Explain how the Board or VA is likely to apply the decision.
avoid: Turning one case into a universal promise.

policy-update.label: Policy Update
policy-update.editorialIntent: Explain what changed, who it affects, and what to do now.
policy-update.toneEmphasis: Urgent where necessary, but procedural and concrete.
mustDo: Show the before-versus-after change.
Call out timing, deadlines, and affected cohorts.
Link the change to actual filing or appeal behavior.
avoid: Generic bureaucratic summaries with no practical takeaway.

claims-strategy.label: Claims Strategy
claims-strategy.editorialIntent: Turn doctrine and evidence rules into an executable filing strategy.
mustDo: Spell out the evidence sequence.
Show the likely VA weak point in the claim.
End with specific action items.
avoid: Abstract advice with no filing or evidence order.

explainer.label: Explainer
explainer.editorialIntent: Teach the rule clearly enough that the reader can use it on a live claim.
explainer.toneEmphasis: Patient and plain-language without dumbing it down.
mustDo: Define the rule or concept early.
Use examples or scenarios.
Correct common misconceptions.
avoid: Assuming the reader already knows VA terminology.

opinion.label: Opinion
opinion.editorialIntent: Make a sharp case while staying grounded in evidence.
mustDo: State the thesis early.
Support assertions with real cases, regs, or data.
Acknowledge the strongest counterargument.

news.label: News
news.editorialIntent: Deliver the event quickly, then explain why veterans should care.
mustDo: State what happened in the first paragraph.
Identify the affected population.
Say whether the reader needs to act now.
